   103→    return allContactLinks.filter(link => link.contact_id === contactId);
   104→  };
   105→
   106→  // Get linked opportunities for a contact
   107→  const getLinkedOpportunities = (contactId: string) => {
   108→    const links = getContactLinks(contactId).filter(l => l.opportunity_id);
   109→    return links.map(l => opportunities.find(o => o.id === l.opportunity_id)).filter(Boolean) as CRMOpportunity[];
   110→  };
   111→
   112→  // Get linked partners for a contact
   113→  const getLinkedPartners = (contactId: string) => {
   114→    const links = getContactLinks(contactId).filter(l => l.partner_id);
   115→    return links.map(l => partners.find(p => p.id === l.partner_id)).filter(Boolean) as CRMPartner[];
   116→  };
   117→
   118→  // Get linked affiliates for a contact
   119→  const getLinkedAffiliates = (contactId: string) => {
   120→    const links = getContactLinks(contactId).filter(l => l.affiliate_id);
   121→    return links.map(l => affiliates.find(a => a.id === l.affiliate_id)).filter(Boolean) as CRMAffiliate[];
   122→  };
   123→
   124→  const handleCreateContact = async () => {
   125→    if (!contactForm.name.trim()) return;
   126→    setIsSubmitting(true);
   127→    try {
   128→      let contactId: string;
   129→
   130→      if (editingContact) {
   131→        await CRMService.updateContact(editingContact.id, contactForm);
   132→        contactId = editingContact.id;
   133→      } else {
   134→        const newContact = await CRMService.createContact({
   135→          ...contactForm,
   136→          owner_id: user?.id
   137→        });
   138→        contactId = newContact.id;
   139→      }
   140→
   141→      // Handle affiliate link
   142→      if (linkToAffiliate && selectedAffiliateId) {
   143→        if (existingAffiliateLink) {
   144→          // If affiliate changed, delete old and create new
   145→          if (existingAffiliateLink.affiliate_id !== selectedAffiliateId) {
   146→            await CRMService.unlinkContact(existingAffiliateLink.id);
   147→            await CRMService.linkContactToAffiliate(contactId, selectedAffiliateId);
   148→          }
   149→        } else {
   150→          await CRMService.linkContactToAffiliate(contactId, selectedAffiliateId);
```

  1775→                                  <Input
  1776→                                    type="number"
  1777→                                    value={editingValue}
  1778→                                    onChange={e => setEditingValue(e.target.value)}
  1779→                                    onBlur={() => handleInlineEdit(opp.id, 'deal_value', editingValue)}
  1780→                                    onKeyDown={e => { if (e.key === 'Enter') handleInlineEdit(opp.id, 'deal_value', editingValue); if (e.key === 'Escape') setEditingCell(null); }}
  1781→                                    className="h-8 text-sm text-right auth-input"
  1782→                                    autoFocus
  1783→                                    onClick={e => e.stopPropagation()}
  1784→                                  />
  1785→                                ) : (
  1786→                                  <div
  1787→                                    onClick={e => { e.stopPropagation(); setEditingCell({ id: opp.id, field: 'deal_value' }); setEditingValue(String(opp.deal_value || '')); }}
  1788→                                    className="cursor-pointer hover:bg-gray-100 rounded px-2 py-1 -mx-2 -my-1 min-h-[28px] flex items-center"
  1789→                                  >
  1790→                                    {opp.deal_value ? (
  1791→                                      <span className="font-semibold text-emerald-600">${opp.deal_value.toLocaleString()}</span>
  1792→                                    ) : (
  1793→                                      <span className="text-gray-400">-</span>
  1794→                                    )}
  1795→                                  </div>
  1796→                                )}
  1797→                              </TableCell>
  1798→                              <TableCell>{getUserName(opp.owner_id)}</TableCell>
  1799→                              <TableCell className="text-gray-500">{opp.tg_handle || '—'}</TableCell>
```
</details></tool-use>

   167→
   168→  const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {
   169→    e.preventDefault();
   170→    if (!selectedDate || !selectedSlot || !bookingPage) return;
   171→
   172→    setSubmitting(true);
   173→    try {
   174→      await BookingService.createBooking({
   175→        booking_page_id: bookingPage.id,
   176→        opportunity_id: oppId || undefined,
   177→        booker_name: bookerName.trim(),
   178→        booker_email: bookerEmail.trim(),

   180→        start_time: selectedSlot.start,
   181→        end_time: selectedSlot.end,
   182→        notes: notes.trim() || undefined,
   183→      });
   184→      setStep('success');
   185→    } catch (err) {
   186→      console.error('Error creating booking:', err);
   187→    } finally {
   188→      setSubmitting(false);
   189→    }
   190→  };
   191→
   192→  /** Format a slot time for display in the selected timezone */
   193→  const formatSlotLocal = (utcTime: string): string => {
   194→    if (!selectedDate) return utcTime;
   195→    return utcTimeToLocal(formatDateKey(selectedDate), utcTime, timezone);
   196→  };
   197→
   198→  /** Format slot with UTC reference */
   199→  const formatSlotWithUtc = (utcTime: string): { local: string; utc: string } => {
   200→    return {
   201→      local: selectedDate ? utcTimeToLocal(formatDateKey(selectedDate), utcTime, timezone) : utcTime,
   202→      utc: utcTime + ' UTC',
   203→    };
   204→  };
   205→
   206→  // Loading state
   207→  if (loading) {
   208→    return (
   209→      <div className="min-h-screen bg-white flex items-center justify-center">
   210→        <div className="text-center">
   211→          <Loader className="h-12 w-12 animate-spin text-[#3e8692] mx-auto mb-4" />
   212→          <p className="text-gray-600 text-lg font-medium">Loading...</p>
   213→        </div>
   214→      </div>
   215→    );
   216→  }
   217→
   218→  // Error state
   219→  if (error || !bookingPage) {
   220→    return (
   221→      <div className="min-h-screen bg-white flex items-center justify-center px-6">
   222→        <div className="text-center max-w-md">
   223→          <div className="flex justify-center mb-6">
   224→            <Image src="/images/logo.png" alt="Logo" width={60} height={60} className="rounded-xl" />

   226→          <div className="rounded-full bg-red-50 p-4 w-20 h-20 mx-auto mb-6 flex items-center justify-center">
   227→            <Calendar className="h-10 w-10 text-red-500" />
   228→          </div>
   229→          <h2 className="text-3xl font-bold text-gray-900 mb-4">Page Not Available</h2>
   230→          <p className="text-lg text-gray-600 leading-relaxed">{error || 'This booking page does not exist.'}</p>
   231→        </div>
   232→      </div>
   233→    );
   234→  }
   235→
   236→  // Success state
   237→  if (step === 'success') {

   239→    const endFmt = selectedSlot ? formatSlotWithUtc(selectedSlot.end) : null;

   246→          <div className="rounded-full bg-green-50 p-4 w-20 h-20 mx-auto mb-6 flex items-center justify-center">

   248→          </div>
   249→          <h2 className="text-3xl font-bold text-gray-900 mb-4">Meeting Booked!</h2>
   250→          <div className="bg-gray-50 rounded-xl p-6 text-left space-y-3 mb-6">
   251→            <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
   252→              <Calendar className="h-5 w-5 text-[#3e8692] flex-shrink-0" />
   253→              <span className="text-gray-700 font-medium">
   254→                {selectedDate && `${DAYS[selectedDate.getDay()]}, ${MONTHS[selectedDate.getMonth()]} ${selectedDate.getDate()}, ${selectedDate.getFullYear()}`}
   255→              </span>
   256→            </div>
   257→            <div className="flex items-center gap-3">

   263→                </div>
   264→                <div className="text-gray-400 text-xs mt-0.5">
   265→                  {startFmt?.utc} - {endFmt?.utc}
   266→                </div>
   267→              </div>
   268→            </div>
   269→            <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
   270→              <User className="h-5 w-5 text-[#3e8692] flex-shrink-0" />
   271→              <span className="text-gray-700 font-medium">{bookerName}</span>
   272→            </div>
   273→          </div>
   274→          <div className="flex items-center gap-2 justify-center text-sm text-gray-500 bg-blue-50 rounded-lg px-4 py-3">

   290→        <div className="text-center mb-12">

   295→          {/* Owner avatar */}

   312→          </h1>
   313→          {bookingPage.description && (

   337→
   338→          {/* Step: Date picker */}
   339→          {step === 'date' && (() => {
   340→            const { year, month } = calendarMonth;
   341→            const firstDay = new Date(year, month, 1);
   342→            const lastDay = new Date(year, month + 1, 0);
   343→            const startDow = firstDay.getDay();
   344→            const daysInMonth = lastDay.getDate();
   345→            const todayKey = formatDateKey(new Date());
   346→            const isCurrentMonth = year === today.getFullYear() && month === today.getMonth();
   347→
   348→            return (
   349→              <div>
   350→                <h2 className="text-lg font-semibold text-gray-800 mb-4 text-center">Select a date</h2>
   351→
   352→                {/* Month navigation */}
   353→                <div className="flex items-center justify-between mb-4">
   354→                  <button
   355→                    onClick={() => {
   356→                      if (isCurrentMonth) return;
   357→                      setCalendarMonth(prev => {
   358→                        const d = new Date(prev.year, prev.month - 1, 1);
   359→                        return { year: d.getFullYear(), month: d.getMonth() };
   360→                      });
   361→                    }}
   362→                    disabled={isCurrentMonth}
   363→                    className={`p-2 rounded-lg transition-colors ${isCurrentMonth ? 'text-gray-200 cursor-not-allowed' : 'text-gray-500 hover:bg-gray-100'}`}
   364→                  >
   365→                    <ChevronLeft className="h-5 w-5" />
   366→                  </button>
   367→                  <span className="text-base font-medium text-gray-700">
   368→                    {MONTHS[month]} {year}
   369→                  </span>
   370→                  <button
   371→                    onClick={() => {
   372→                      setCalendarMonth(prev => {
   373→                        const d = new Date(prev.year, prev.month + 1, 1);
   374→                        return { year: d.getFullYear(), month: d.getMonth() };
   375→                      });
   376→                    }}
   377→                    className="p-2 rounded-lg text-gray-500 hover:bg-gray-100 transition-colors"
   378→                  >
   379→                    <ChevronRight className="h-5 w-5" />
   380→                  </button>
   381→                </div>
   382→
   383→                <div className="grid grid-cols-7 gap-2">
   384→                  {/* Day headers */}
   385→                  {DAYS.map(d => (
   386→                    <div key={d} className="text-center text-xs font-medium text-gray-400 pb-2">{d}</div>
   387→                  ))}
   388→
   389→                  {/* Empty cells before first day */}
   390→                  {Array.from({ length: startDow }).map((_, i) => (
   391→                    <div key={`empty-${i}`} />
   392→                  ))}
   393→
   394→                  {/* Date cells */}
   395→                  {Array.from({ length: daysInMonth }, (_, i) => {
   396→                    const date = new Date(year, month, i + 1);
   397→                    const dateKey = formatDateKey(date);
   398→                    const dayOfWeek = date.getDay();
   399→                    const hasSlots = bookingPage.available_slots.some(s => s.day === dayOfWeek);
   400→                    const isPast = dateKey < todayKey;
   401→                    const isDisabled = !hasSlots || isPast;
   402→                    const isToday = dateKey === todayKey;
   403→
   404→                    return (
   405→                      <button
   406→                        key={dateKey}
   407→                        onClick={() => !isDisabled && handleDateSelect(date)}
   408→                        disabled={isDisabled}
   409→                        className={`
   410→                          aspect-square rounded-lg flex items-center justify-center text-sm transition-all
   411→                          ${isDisabled
   412→                            ? 'text-gray-300 cursor-not-allowed'
   413→                            : 'hover:bg-[#3e8692] hover:text-white cursor-pointer border border-gray-200 hover:border-[#3e8692]'
   414→                          }
   415→                          ${isToday ? 'font-bold ring-2 ring-[#3e8692]/30' : ''}
   416→                        `}
   417→                      >
   418→                        <span className="text-lg">{i + 1}</span>
   419→                      </button>
   420→                    );
   421→                  })}
   422→                </div>
   423→              </div>
   424→            );
   425→          })()}
   426→
   427→          {/* Step: Time slots */}

Perfect! Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive exploration report. Let me compile my findings:

## Comprehensive Codebase Exploration Report

Based on my thorough exploration of the KOL Campaign Manager codebase, here's what I've found:

### 1. **Sidebar Navigation Structure**

**Location:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/Sidebar.tsx`

The sidebar is a collapsible, section-based navigation component organized into logical groups:

- **Top Section:** Holo GPT (Chat)
- **People Section:** Team, Clients
- **KOLs Section:** KOLs, Lists, Campaigns
- **CRM Section:** Sales, Network, Contacts, Submissions, TG Chats (admin-only)
- **Workspace Section:** Daily Stand-Up, Tasks (Andy-only for testing)
- **Documents Section:** Delivery Logs, Forms (admin-only), Links, Templates, SOPs (admin-only)
- **Admin Section:** Field Options, Changelog (super admin), Archive

Navigation items are defined as `Link` components using the `Button` component with dynamic styling based on `pathname`. The sidebar supports collapsing/expanding with localStorage persistence. Icon-based navigation is shown in collapsed mode with tooltips.

### 2. **Page Structure in App Directory**

**Key Patterns Observed:**

- **Layout pattern:** Each major section (crm, campaigns, clients, kols) has a `layout.tsx` and `page.tsx`
- **List pages** (e.g., `/app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx`, `/app/kols/page.tsx`):
  - Use `'use client'` directive
  - State management for: loading, data array, filters, search, view modes (table/kanban/cards)
  - Dialog/Modal components for create/edit operations
  - Table and card-based renderings with inline editing
  - Dropdown menus for row actions
  
- **Detail pages** (e.g., `/app/campaigns/[id]/page.tsx`):
  - Use dynamic route parameters `[id]`
  - Implement tabbed interfaces (Tabs component)
  - Support both view and edit modes
  - Include sub-tables for related items
  - Support copy/paste functionality with cell selection
  - Use sticky scrollbars for horizontal scrolling

### 3. **Booking-Related Code Architecture**

**BookingService** (`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/bookingService.ts`):

Key interfaces:
```typescript
interface Booking {
  id: string;
  booking_page_id: string;
  opportunity_id: string | null;
  booker_name: string;
  booker_email: string;
  meeting_date: string; // YYYY-MM-DD
  start_time: string; // HH:MM:SS
  end_time: string; // HH:MM:SS
  status: 'confirmed' | 'cancelled';
  notes: string | null;
  created_at: string;
}

interface BookingPage {
  id: string;
  user_id: string;
  slug: string;
  title: string | null;
  description: string | null;
  slot_duration_minutes: number;
  available_slots: AvailableSlot[]; // {day: number, start: string, end: string}
  is_active: boolean;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
}
```

Key methods available:
- `getBookingPageBySlug(slug)` - Public, no auth required
- `getAvailableSlots(pageId, date, slots, duration)` - Computes available time slots minus booked slots
- `createBooking(data)` - Public, creates a booking and triggers database trigger to update opportunity
- `getBookingsByPage(pageId, startDate?, endDate?)` - Get bookings for a page
- `getMyBookingPage()` - Authenticated, get current user's booking page
- `updateBookingPage(id, updates)` - Authenticated, update booking page settings
- `cancelBooking(id)` - **Status update pattern: Sets status to 'cancelled'**
- `getBookingPageByUserId(userId)` - Get booking page for generating links in pipeline

### 4. **Existing Calendar/Scheduling UI Components**

**Public Booking Page** (`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/public/book/[slug]/page.tsx`):

- **Multi-step form flow:** date → time → form → success
- **Date picker:** Month navigation with disabled past dates
- **Time slots:** Grid display with timezone-aware formatting
- **Timezone selector:** 20+ timezone options with automatic user timezone detection
- **Booking form:** Name, email, notes fields
- **Success confirmation:** Displays booked date/time with UTC reference

**Settings/Booking Management** (`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/settings/page.tsx`):

- Authenticated users can configure their booking page
- Set title, description, slug
- Configure available slots per day
- Set slot duration (in minutes)
- Toggle booking page active/inactive status

### 5. **List/Detail Page Status Update Patterns**

**Sales Pipeline Page** (`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx`):

The page implements comprehensive status/stage management patterns:

**Dialog-based operations:**
- Create dialog: `isCreateOpen` state with form
- Edit dialog/slide-over: `slideOverOpp` state with slide-over mode
- Delete with confirmation: `handleDelete()` function
- Stage change prompts: `orbitPrompt`, `closedLostPrompt`, `bucketPrompt` states

**Inline editing pattern:**
- `editingCell` state tracks which cell is being edited: `{id: string, field: string}`
- `editingValue` stores the temporary input value
- Double-click or click-to-edit triggers edit mode
- `onBlur` or Enter key calls `handleInlineEdit()` to save
- Escape key cancels editing

**Status update methods:**
- `handleStageChange(oppId, newStage, oldStage)` - Moves opportunity between stages
- May trigger secondary prompts (e.g., asking for reason when moving to "orbit")
- `handleDelete(oppId)` - Deletes an opportunity
- Activities are logged: `setActivityLogPrompt()` triggers a dialog to record actions

**Dropdown menu for row actions:**
```
- Edit
- Record Bump (for cold_dm stage)
- Move to Orbit
- Delete
```

**Other status-like operations:**
- `handleRecordBump()` - Records activity
- `handleInlineEdit()` - Saves single field changes
- `handleDragEnd()` - Moves opportunities between stages via drag-and-drop

**Contacts Page** (`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/crm/contacts/page.tsx`):

Similar patterns for contact operations:
- Create/Edit dialogs with form state
- Delete confirmation dialog
- Linking to other entities (affiliates, partners)
- Filter and search state management

### 6. **Key Architectural Patterns**

**State Management:**
- React hooks (useState, useEffect, useCallback, useMemo)
- No global state management library (Redux, Zustand, etc.)
- Local state for UI (loading, editing, visibility)
- Service classes for data operations

**Data Flow:**
- Service classes (BookingService, CRMService, etc.) handle all database operations
- Pages call service methods in useEffect for initial data fetch
- CRUD operations go through service methods
- Toast notifications for user feedback (`useToast()`)

**UI Patterns:**
- Dialogs for modal operations (create, edit, delete confirmation)
- Dropdowns for action menus
- Tables with inline editing
- Cards for list views
- Tabs for organizing content
- Skeleton loaders for loading states

**Validation & Error Handling:**
- Basic required field validation before submit
- Error alerts/toasts
- Try-catch blocks in service methods

### 7. **Related File Locations**

- UI Components: `/components/ui/*` (Shadcn components)
- Services: `/lib/*Service.ts` (CRMService, SalesPipelineService, BookingService, etc.)
- Contexts: `/contexts/AuthContext.tsx`
- Hooks: `/hooks/use-toast.ts`
- API Routes: `/app/api/*` (Telegraph webhook, forms, etc.)

This architecture provides a solid foundation for implementing a bookings management interface with list views, detail dialogs, and status management capabilities.

     1→# Booking Confirmation Email + Google Meet Integration
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→When someone books a meeting via the public booking page, nothing happens beyond a success screen. We need to:
     5→1. Auto-create a Google Meet link for the meeting
     6→2. Send a confirmation email to the booker with meeting details + Meet link
     7→3. Also email the booking page owner (team member)
     8→
     9→## Prerequisites (Manual Setup Steps)
    10→
    11→### Step 1: Create a Resend account
    12→1. Go to https://resend.com and sign up
    13→2. Go to **API Keys** → Create a new key → copy it
    14→3. Go to **Domains** → Add `holohive.io` → add the DNS records (MX, TXT) they provide
    15→4. Wait for domain verification (usually a few minutes)
    16→
    17→### Step 2: Create a Google Cloud service account
    18→1. Go to https://console.cloud.google.com
    19→2. Create a new project (or use existing) — e.g. "HoloHive Bookings"
    20→3. Enable the **Google Calendar API**: APIs & Services → Library → search "Google Calendar API" → Enable
    21→4. Create a service account: APIs & Services → Credentials → Create Credentials → Service Account
    22→   - Name: "holohive-bookings"
    23→   - Grant no extra roles, just create it
    24→5. Click the service account → Keys tab → Add Key → JSON → Download the JSON file
    25→6. **Important**: Each team member must share their Google Calendar with the service account email (e.g. `holohive-bookings@project-id.iam.gserviceaccount.com`) — give "Make changes to events" permission
    26→
    27→### Step 3: Add secrets to Supabase
    28→In Supabase Dashboard → Edge Functions → Secrets, add:
    29→- `RESEND_API_KEY` — your Resend API key
    30→- `GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON` — the entire JSON file contents from step 2
    31→- `GOOGLE_CALENDAR_ID` — (optional) defaults to `primary`; each team member's calendar email can be used
    32→
    33→Also add the team member calendar mapping as a secret:
    34→- `BOOKING_CALENDAR_MAP` — JSON mapping of user emails to calendar IDs, e.g. `{"yano@holohive.io":"yano@holohive.io","andy@holohive.io":"andy@holohive.io"}`
    35→
    36→## Database Migration
    37→
    38→Add columns to `bookings`:
    39→- `meet_link` (text, nullable) — Google Meet URL
    40→- `google_event_id` (text, nullable) — for future cancellation/updates
    41→- `confirmation_sent` (boolean, default false) — track email status
    42→
    43→## Edge Function: `booking-confirmation`
    44→
    45→**Trigger:** Called by DB trigger via `pg_net` on bookings INSERT.
    46→
    47→**Flow:**
    48→1. Receive booking data (booking ID)
    49→2. Fetch full booking details + booking page + owner from DB
    50→3. Create Google Calendar event with Meet link:
    51→   - Use service account JWT auth (sign JWT from JSON key, exchange for access token)
    52→   - POST to `https://www.googleapis.com/calendar/v3/calendars/{calendarId}/events?conferenceDataVersion=1`
    53→   - Include `conferenceData.createRequest` to auto-generate Meet link
    54→   - Add booker as attendee
    55→4. Update booking row with `meet_link` and `google_event_id`
    56→5. Send confirmation email to booker via Resend
    57→6. Send notification email to team member (booking page owner) via Resend
    58→7. Return success
    59→
    60→**Files:**
    61→- `supabase/functions/booking-confirmation/index.ts` — main Edge Function
    62→
    63→**Email template (booker):**
    64→- Subject: "Meeting Confirmed — {page_title}"
    65→- Body: Meeting date/time (UTC), duration, Meet link button, team member name, notes
    66→
    67→**Email template (owner):**
    68→- Subject: "New Booking — {booker_name}"
    69→- Body: Booker name/email, meeting date/time, opportunity name if linked, Meet link
    70→
    71→## DB Trigger Update
    72→
    73→Replace the broken `notify_new_booking` trigger (currently uses `pg_notify` which nobody listens to) with a proper `net.http_post()` call to the Edge Function:
    74→
    75→```sql
    76→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION notify_new_booking()
    77→RETURNS trigger AS $$
    78→BEGIN
    79→  PERFORM net.http_post(
    80→    url := '<SUPABASE_URL>/functions/v1/booking-confirmation',
    81→    headers := jsonb_build_object(
    82→      'Content-Type', 'application/json',
    83→      'Authorization', 'Bearer <SERVICE_ROLE_KEY>'
    84→    ),
    85→    body := jsonb_build_object('booking_id', NEW.id)
    86→  );
    87→  RETURN NEW;
    88→END;
    89→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;
    90→```
    91→
    92→The actual URL and key will be fetched from `supabase_vault` secrets (not hardcoded).
    93→
    94→## Files to Create/Modify

    97→- Google Calendar event creation with Meet link
    98→- Resend email to booker + owner
    99→- Updates `bookings` row with meet_link, google_event_id, confirmation_sent
   100→
   101→### 2. DB Migration: `add_booking_email_columns` (via MCP)
   102→- Add `meet_link`, `google_event_id`, `confirmation_sent` to bookings
   103→- Replace `notify_new_booking()` trigger to call Edge Function via `pg_net`

   114→6. Verify: booker receives confirmation email with Meet link

```md
# Meetings Page — View & Cancel Bookings

## Context
Users need an internal page to view all their booked meetings, see Google Meet links, and cancel meetings. Currently bookings are only visible in the database — there's no UI for team members to manage them.

## Approach
Add a "Meetings" sidebar item under the **CRM section** (next to Sales, Network, etc.) that shows a table of all bookings for the logged-in user's booking page(s), with the ability to cancel.

## Files to Modify

### 1. `components/Sidebar.tsx` — Add "Meetings" nav item
- Add under CRM section, after "Submissions"
- Icon: `Calendar` from lucide-react
- Path: `/crm/meetings`

### 2. `app/crm/meetings/page.tsx` — NEW page
- Fetch bookings for the current user's booking page(s) via `BookingService`
- Table columns: Date, Time (UTC), Booker Name, Booker Email, Status, Meet Link, Notes, Opportunity
- Status badge: `confirmed` (green) / `cancelled` (red)
- Row actions dropdown: Cancel (with confirmation dialog), Copy Meet Link
- Meet Link column: clickable "Join" button that opens Google Meet
- Filter tabs: Upcoming / Past / Cancelled
- Default sort: upcoming meetings first (by date ascending)

### 3. `lib/bookingService.ts` — Add methods
- `getMyBookings()` — fetch all bookings for the current user's booking page(s), joined with booking_pages and optionally crm_opportunities for the opportunity name
- Update `Booking` interface to include `meet_link`, `google_event_id`, `confirmation_sent`

## UI Pattern
Follow the existing CRM page patterns:
- Loading skeleton while fetching
- Empty state when no bookings
- `useToast()` for cancel confirmation feedback
- Dialog for cancel confirmation ("Are you sure?")
- Tab filters using simple button group (Upcoming / Past / Cancelled)

## Verification
1. Sidebar shows "Meetings" under CRM
2. Page loads and shows bookings in a table
3. Cancel button opens confirmation dialog, sets status to `cancelled`
4. Meet link buttons open Google Meet in new tab
5. Tabs filter correctly between upcoming/past/cancelled

```
